title: Fact frontmatter
_comment: Schema for fact primitives. A fact is a verifiable claim about the world (a person's role, a metric, a policy). Downstream consumers (insights skill, future fact-rebuild aggregator) depend on these fields. Malformed YAML silently drops the entry from any rebuild pass. Includes the cross-type frontmatter contract (provenance, confidence, freshness_days, last_verified, source_count, memory_class, entity_ids) shared across all 8 typed memory primitives. memory_class default for fact is procedural. entity_ids cross-source linking (slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear).

claim.description: The factual statement, written as a complete sentence.

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the source was captured.

provenance.description: List of independent sources that back this entry.

confidence.description: How sure the system is the entry is accurate, 0.0 to 1.0.

freshness_days.description: How many days the entry stays fresh before re-verification is recommended.

last_verified.description: ISO 8601 date when the entry was last confirmed by a human or a re-extraction pass.

source_count.description: How many independent sources back this entry.

memory_class.description: Episodic captures one-time events; procedural captures repeatable, time-stable knowledge. Default for fact is procedural.

entity_ids.description: Cross-source identifiers. Keys: slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear. Values: source-system IDs.
